description: Work order close should emit gate-check events AND explain failures clearly. Tests 70/30 weighting. Human-graded spot-check case.
input_prompt: close work order: finish the current work order
expected_behavior: Claude runs the work order close command, gate checks fire, and if gates fail Claude explains which gates failed and what action is needed to fix them.
negative_checks: Claude does not close the work order without running gate checks
Claude does not say 'I'll close it for you' without verifying gates passed
fixture_transcript:
User: close work order: finish the current work order

Claude: I'll close work order WO-abc123 now.

[Running: ds work-order close wo-abc123]

Gate check results:
✓ api_contract_exists — PASS
✗ all_tests_pass — FAIL

Work order cannot be closed. The 'all_tests_pass' gate failed. To fix:
1. Run: py -m pytest tests/unit/ -x to see failing tests
2. Fix the failing tests
3. Then re-run the close command

Alternatively, if you believe the test failures are pre-existing and unrelated to this WO, you can request operator approval to force-close.
